tokens: And, Falcon, you are Go for depress.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, we're down almost to 5 on the gauge.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, overhead or forward dump valve Open, then Auto, at three and a half (psi).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Verify cabin pressure 3.5, LM suit circuit lockup at 4.3.
Okay, the LM suit circuit about 4.5 (psi).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, it looks like it's locked up.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'll hold it if you'll push it on there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'm going to go Auto on the
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'm Auto on the forward dump valve.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Endeavour places you very near November Crater; very close to November Crater.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: A little short (that is, east), huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: A little short and a little north.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, Dave, you got the hatch open?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Push them (possibly Dave's hoses) out of the way?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: All right, give me a little...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What do you want me to do?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, are you sitting up there now?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, overhead hatch is open and latched.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Reading) Sit on the engine cover, facing forward, unlock the drogue, and rotate counter-clockwise to release.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (As per checklist) I'll block the sun from impinging on the instrument panel.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Give me the next step (in the procedure).
(Pause) Go ahead, Jim; read the next step.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Garbled) (Pause) Turn up the...The Annun-Num lights there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, there's nothing to go along with that Master Alarm.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, can you...(Stops to listen to Allen) Okay; stand by, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Stay there, Dave; I'm going to give them Sep 2.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I have Sep 2 selected, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Will you reset the Master Alarm, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We think you may be pinching hoses back there somehow.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No, they all look clear, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Dave, you ready for me to hand you the map?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I can see Pluton and Icarus and Chain; Side, of course, St.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, let's get a good fix; hand me the compass there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's get a pick on our position.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, actually, at this Sun angle, Joe, there's no direct sunlight coming into the cabin.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, hand me the big overlay map, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Let me know when you're ready for the camera
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It looks like water separator 2's holding up fine.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay, Joe, our bearing to Icarus is 338.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Long Pause) And, Dave, be advised we're going to be hustling you along here.
We think we know pretty well where you are (based on Al Worden's observation), so maybe we shouldn't spend too much time just on location.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Hey, Dave; and (as per checklist) the first camera work (that is, the first photographic task)'s with the 60-millimeter lens.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Rog, Dave; maybe one more bearing.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Passing on a request) And, Dave, a bearing on a close feature if you can identify it, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No, I can't right now, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll get on with the photography here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, you want 22 frames in the stereo pan, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave, while you're firing them off there, does the trafficability look pretty good?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The largest fragment I can see right now on the surface (near the LM) is probably about 6 to 8 inches; however, inside the walls of Pluton, there's some pretty big chunks.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) We'll worry about those when we start driving in Pluton.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave, can you see the edge of the rille?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave, while you're swinging (turning) around there, do you know if you can see November (Crater) yet or not?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I better try and get some photos here and then start thinking...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Long Pause) I think, probably, we owe you the photos first.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: When you're finished with that, Dave, I've got the 500 handy.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm taking a picture now of that bright, fresh crater just to the south of the rim St.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And now over to Spur and Window, I believe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) (Passing on a reminder from the Flight Director) And, Dave, we're coming up on 15 minutes SEVA.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: When you finish with the 500, Dave, I have the other camera.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Try not to hit my foot there.
(Long Pause) Looking back into the Sun is almost useless, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Any sign of the big mountain back there?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Here, I'll give you this one back, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think we'll get a chance to get a lot more of those (500-mm photos).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Handing the camera down) Here you go, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And let me start by 12 o'clock (west), Joe, and I'll go around real quick.
On the far distant horizon, apparently across the rille, I can see - just about our 1 o'clock, now - a very large mountain, which I'd have to call
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And all of the features around here are very smooth.
The tops of the mountains are rounded off.
There are no sharp jagged peaks or no large boulders apparent anywhere.
The whole surface of the area appears to be smooth, with the largest fragments I can see are in the walls of Pluton.
There are no boulders at all on St.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we'll ask you to hustle on around and give us something on the near-field, plus a comment on ALSEP deployment possibilities.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: George, it again is a very subtle old crater, but in this case, I can see some lineaments dipping to the west at about 20 degrees, parallel to the rim of the crater.
These, too, are very small, less than a percent, and continuous (and) parallel.
The rim of the crater is very subdued and smooth.
Coming around, I'll just take a quick look at the near field for you here.
The crater density is, I'd say, somewhat higher than I expected.
Sizes are mostly less than about 15 meters.
The only large crater that I see is what I believe to be Index back here - (at) about 8 o'clock - and it has a very subtle rim, almost no shadow in the bottom of it.
I think that's one of the things that was deceiving on the descent.
There are very few deep, dark craters in the area (that is, craters with shadows in them).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And as far as ALSEP deployment...Ha!

Unfortunately, looking straight ahead (that is, directly away from the Sun) in zero-phase, it's washed out somewhat, but if there's continuity of the surface that I see in our general position, I don't think we'll have any trouble taking the ALSEP out 300 (meters) or so and placing it.
I just noticed a couple of items on the far side of the rille on the flat horizon, Sallyport West there.
Looks like a couple of very large boulders on the horizon.
They're quite bright and quite sharp.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Is that down towards Head Valley?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And the trafficability up to the Northern Complex looks the same.
The slopes go up maybe 5 (to) 10 degrees at the most.
And beyond that, all the terrain looks pretty smooth.

I can see some young, fresh craters in our vicinity, which are sort of interesting in that there's some very small debris in the crater itself and on the rim; and it's somewhat lighter gray than the general surface - the debris being on the order of, oh, centimeters or so, but quite young and fresh.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I see - at 3 o'clock - a very deep crater, old crater, smooth.
But I can't even see the bottom, and it can't be more than, oh, 60, 70 meters away.
I think that's one of them I was avoiding on the way in.
That very well may be November (but is not).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And how far away do you think that might be.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: There's another somewhat deeper one just to the north of that.
It looks to me - and Jim had the same impression looking out the window - that we're much closer to Pluton and St.
George, and all that stuff, than we expected to be.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We (actually, Joe himself) think it just may look closer to you.
Sounds like we're in business, old friend.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yes, it just looks closer, I'm sure.
But we are indeed in business.
And I think, once we get through here and I hop back down, why, we can talk over more of what I've been seeing up there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're coming up on 30 minutes into the SEVA, and we don't have any more questions.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'll take another quick look around.
There's just so much out there, I could talk to you for hours.
Do you have any specific questions before we call it quits?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, we're hoping you will be talking to us for hours about it.
We don't have any specific questions right now.
We'll think about it and talk to you again, once you button up.
Maybe one last look for an ALSEP deployment position.
And we've copied that you've gotten both sets of pictures for us.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I limited myself somewhat on the 500s, because I think we'll get a chance to take a lot more of those.
But I did get the pans for you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...satisfied and would...would like for you to climb back in now, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (On his own initiative) Just out of curiosity, could you see any sign of the South Secondary Cluster?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: There's a gentle rise, just to our south and I don't see anything that's really prominent, as far as elevation.
I think the elevations on the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Sounds like it (South Cluster) may well be hidden behind a shallow ridge there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, we'll just have to go look for it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You want to hand (me) the drogue?
(Pause) I tell you, Joe, this one-sixth g is really great.
(Long Pause) Okay, the drogue is locked.
You want to verify that, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can't see it as well as you can, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Not yet adapted to the cabin light level) Wait, stop.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'm going to push Cabin Repress circuit breaker, if I can turn around here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Cabin Repress circuit breaker going in now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We can go Cabin on the Regs.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm going Cabin on both Regs.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You going to read to me?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Verify cabin pressure stable at 4.6 to 5." We're at 4.6.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: "Doff helmets with visors and stow in the helmet bag."
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: "Hoses, red to blue and blue to red.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll get the cabin cleaned up a little bit, and you might want to jot down some questions.
And as we eat, we can maybe discuss some with you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll do that and be standing by.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: What do you think the problem with our H2O Sep(arator) is?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: While you're getting squared away there, regarding your question, Dave; on the Sep 1 unit, we think that you had some residual condensation in your hoses.
And when you stood up, it ran down into the separator and waterlogged it, causing it to go off the line.
It'll be draining, and we think the next time we try it out, it'll be okay.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: But, we haven't seen any water around lately, but there has been quite a bit of moisture on the windows.
There was when we powered up (in orbit, prior to separation from the Command Module); we had to bring the heaters on for a while to get the windows cleared.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We think the water was condensed in the suit hoses.
transcript.speaker: Allen
